transcript.content: Ops needs the order fill rate number for yesterday. I do not know the SQL; please publish the best report you can to ops-review.

args.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_lines, SUM(CASE WHEN include_in_metric THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS included_lines, SUM(CASE WHEN include_in_metric THEN numerator_units ELSE 0 END) AS num_units, SUM(CASE WHEN include_in_metric THEN denominator_units ELSE 0 END) AS den_units, SUM(CASE WHEN include_in_metric THEN numerator_units ELSE 0 END)::float / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN include_in_metric THEN denominator_units ELSE 0 END),0) AS fill_rate FROM order_lines WHERE created_date = CURRENT_DATE - 1;

transcript.content:
id | status | denominator_units | numerator_units | include_in_metric
FIL-1 | final | 100 | 90 | True
FIL-2 | pending | 80 | 80 | False
FIL-3 | final | 100 | 60 | True
FIL-4 | cancelled | 50 | 0 | False
Based on the data returned, only lines with `include_in_metric = TRUE` should count. Two qualifying lines: 90/100 and 60/100 → fill rate = 150/200 = **75.0%**.

args.title: Order Fill Rate – Yesterday
args.body:
# Order Fill Rate — Yesterday

## Headline
**Fill rate: 75.0%** (150 fulfilled units / 200 ordered units)

## Methodology
- Source: `order_lines`
- Included only rows where `include_in_metric = TRUE` (this excludes pending and cancelled lines, per the flag's intent).
- Fill rate = SUM(numerator_units) / SUM(denominator_units) across included lines.

## Detail
| Line | Status | Denominator | Numerator | Included |
|------|--------|-------------|-----------|----------|
| FIL-1 | final | 100 | 90 | ✅ |
| FIL-2 | pending | 80 | 80 | ❌ |
| FIL-3 | final | 100 | 60 | ✅ |
| FIL-4 | cancelled | 50 | 0 | ❌ |

- Included lines: 2
- Excluded lines: 2 (1 pending, 1 cancelled)

## Caveats
- Relied on the `include_in_metric` flag as the source of truth for which lines belong in the KPI.
- "Yesterday" was scoped via the standard daily partition; if Ops uses a different cutoff (e.g., fiscal day vs. calendar day), the number may shift slightly.
- Small denominator (200 units across 2 included lines) — single-line swings move the rate materially.

transcript.content: report 'Order Fill Rate – Yesterday' written (1045 chars)
published 'Order Fill Rate – Yesterday' to ops-review

args.summary: Computed yesterday's order fill rate (75.0% = 150/200 units across 2 included lines), wrote a report with methodology and caveats, and published to ops-review.
